flares.note: First peak on a double-peaked flare from two separate regions of the disk, second peak of C4.0 at 2026-06-05T15:13Z.
Second peak on a double-peaked flare from two separate regions of the disk, first peak of C4.1 at 2026-06-05T15:03Z.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the North by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-06-02T03:12Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 in later frames. This event is blocked by the pylon in GOES CCOR-1. The source of this CME is a C1.8 flare from Active Region 14455 (N15E09) seen peaking at 2026-06-02T02:50Z in SDO AIA 131. A small jet of filament ejecta is visible in SDO AIA and GOES SUVI 304 in association with this flare, and is heavily deflected to the North, likely due to this region sitting just North of a coronal hole.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the North by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-06-02T07:24Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 in later frames. This event is blocked by the pylon in GOES CCOR-1. The source of this event is a C3.1 flare from Active Region 14455 (N15E01) which peaked at 2026-06-02T06:59Z as seen in SDO AIA 131. A small jet of filament ejecta is visible in SDO AIA and GOES SUVI 304 in association with this flare, and is heavily deflected to the North, likely due to this region sitting just North of a coronal hole.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.

cmes.note: Bright loop CME first seen to the East by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-06-02T12:12Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. This CME has a faint, diffuse, preceding feature, but not a shock, seen ahead of the main bulk, likely due to the nature of the source eruption. The source of this event is a very gradual filament eruption seen centered at approx. S10E75 as seen in GOES SUVI, STEREO A EUVI and SDO AIA 304. This filament becomes unstable beginning at approx. 2026-06-02T10:30Z and gradually rises off the limb, but does not begin to disconnect from the disk until approx. 2026-06-02T12:00Z. This earlier rise likely accounts for the diffuse feature seen in coronagraphs before the main bulk of the filament ejecta. Field line opening, dimming and post eruptive arcades are also visible in association with this eruption in SDO AIA 171, 193, 211 and GOES SUVI 284.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: Bright CME first seen to the Northeast by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-06-02T17:00Z. The source of this event is a filament eruption seen along or just beyond the NE limb at approx. N18 as seen in SDO AIA and GOES SUVI 304 beginning at approx. 2026-06-02T16:43Z. Wide opening field lines are also visible in association with this eruption in SDO AIA 171, 193 and GOES SUVI 284.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement of the BULK feature based on best fit among SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 as well as on the visible source over the NE limb.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the East in STEREO A COR2 beginning at 2026-06-02T17:23Z. The CME is not clearly seen in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 or GOES CCOR-1 as it is likely obscured by the occulting disk. The source of this event is an M3.3 class flare from Active Region 14455 (N13W05) which peaked at 2026-06-02T16:50Z as seen in SDO AIA 131. Opening field lines and dimming are also observed in association with this flare in SDO AIA 171, 193 and 211 with the dimming and opening facing Southward, likely indicating some Southern deflection.

cmeAnalyses.note: Very speculative measurement based on limited frames from STEREO A COR2 and visible on-disk source.

cmes.note: Narrow, faint CME first seen to the NE in STEREO A COR2 starting around 2026-06-02T18:09Z. This feature is only seen in STEREO A COR2 because it is mostly likely obscured by the occulting disk in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1. The source is most likely some additional outflow following CME: 2026-06-02T17:23Z as there is no independent source signature apart from continuous field line fluctuation following CME: 2026-06-02T17:23Z. This CME fades before reached the end of the FOV of STEREO A COR2.

cmeAnalyses.note: Speculative measurement based on limited imagery from STEREO A COR2 and approximate source location.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the NE in SOHO LASCO C2 starting around 2026-06-02T19:48Z. It is also seen to the NE in SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 but is covered by a real-time data gap in STEREO A COR2. There are two source candidates for this CME. The first and most likely is some faint field line movement from beyond the NE limb in SDO AIA 171 beginning around 2026-06-02T17:30Z following the filament eruption associated with CME: 2026-06-02T17:00Z. The second and less likely candidate is some additional minor ejecta from AR 14455 following CME: 2026-06-02T17:23Z, but measurement indicates that this second source candidate is not a good fit.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement due to lack of available triangulation and uncertain source.

cmes.note: Narrow CME first visible to the NE part way into the FOV of SOHO LASCO C2 staring around 2026-06-02T23:00Z. It is also seen to the NE in SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 but is covered by a real-time data gap in STEREO A COR2. No source candidate has been found on the Earth-facing disk, so the source is most likely far-sided. This front could be additional outflow following CME: 2026-06-02T17:00Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement due to lack of available triangulation and lack of definitive source.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the E in SOHO LASCO C2 starting at 2026-06-02T23:24Z. It is also seen to the E in SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 but is covered by a real-time data gap in STEREO A COR2. No source candidate has been found on the Earth-facing disk, so the source is most likely far-sided.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement due to lack of available triangulation and lack of source candidate.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the E in STEREO A COR2 starting at 2026-06-03T01:53Z. It is also faintly seen as a halo to the NW in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 but is partially obscured by the pylon. It is also seen as a halo to the NW in GOES CCOR-1. This CME is associated with an M9.3 class flare from AR 14455 (N13W10) that peaked at 2026-06-03T01:36Z. The flare can best be seen in SDO AIA 131.

cmeAnalyses.note: Updated measurement based on best fit among SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2.
SHOCK measurement based on best fit of the wider shock feature in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2.
PRELIMINARY measurement based on best fit of early frames among SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the northwest in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source is an eruption visible on/near the western limb in SDO AIA 131/171/193/304, GOES SUVI 131/171/195/284/304, and STEREO A EUVI 195/304 imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on best fit among SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: This CME is visible as a partial halo to the north/northwest in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery, and to the northeast in STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source is an M7.7 flare from Active Region 14455 (N14W13) starting around 2026-06-03T07:00Z as seen in SDO AIA 131/171/194/304, GOES SUVI 131/171/195/284/304 imagery, and STEREO A EUVI 195/304 imagery. A large ejecta is associated with this event which is deflected to the northwest. Additionally, an EUV wave is observed moving northwest of the eruption site, as seen in SDO AIA 171/193 and GOES SUVI 171/195/284 imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Approximate analysis of the messy, two-lobed bulk front of this CME, based on the fit with three coronagraphs (STEREO A COR2, SOHO LASCO and GOES CCOR-1). There is clear northward deflection from the source location of the M-class flare (N14W13) but the associated erupting filament seems to originate a bit to the west from the flare location and is then seen deflecting northwardly, so the coronal signatures generally confirm this analysis. The half-width of the CME is based on later imagery in STEREO A COR2, as in coronagraphs there is/are later, wider, lobe(s) of this CME that seem to be integral part of it.
Approximate analysis of the fainter and wider partial halo front best seen in GOES CCOR-1 but also seen in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and not seen in STEREO A COR2 coronagraph.

cmes.note: This CME is visible as a partial halo to the north/northwest in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery, and to the northeast in STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source is an X1.0 flare and subsequent eruption from Active Region 14455 (N14W16) starting around 2026-06-03T11:20Z as seen in SDO AIA 131/171/193/304, GOES SUVI 131/171/195/284/304, and STEREO A EUVI 195/304 imagery. The associated ejecta displays a southeast deflection as seen in SDO AIA 304 imagery. A faint EUV wave is observed moving northwest of the eruption site, however, in SDO AIA 193 imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: A remeasurement of the two-lobed bulk front with more imagery.

Approximate analysis of the faint partial halo shock front of this CME based on the fit with GOES CCOR-1 coronagraph, in which this front is seen clearer. LASCO imagery is also used. The front is not seen/clear in STEREO A COR2 coronagraph.
Very preliminary, approximate analysis of the two-lobed bulk of the CME with very early imagery based on the fit in coronagraphs.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the SW in SOHO LASCO C2 starting at 2026-06-03T14:02Z. It is also seen to the SW in SOHO LASCO C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2. The source of this CME is most likely some very faint moving field lines from well beyond the SW limb best seen in GOES SUVI 284 starting around 2026-06-03T13:30Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on best fit among SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2.

cmes.note: Faint CME first seen to the SW by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-06-03T20:36Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. This event is not seen in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap which began at 2026-06-03T19:38Z. The source of this event is faint field line movement/opening seen along the SW limb in SDO AIA 171 beginning at approx. 2026-06-3T19:00Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky using longitude of W90 due to source near the limb and lack of STEREO A COR2 imagery to triangulate

cmes.note: Faint CME first seen to the West by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-06-03T23:00Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. This event is not seen in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap which began at 2026-06-03T19:38Z. The source of this event is a small eruption seen beginning at approx. 2026-06-3T22:30Z from the vicinity of Active Region 14446 (S15W73) as seen in SDO AIA 171/193/304.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2 and GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the NW in SOHO LASCO C2 starting at 2026-06-04T04:24Z. This front fades quickly. It is no longer visible by the time it reaches the edge of the FOV of SOHO LASCO C2, so it is only seen in SOHO LASCO C2 and very faintly for a couple frames in STEREO A COR2. The source of this CME is most likely field line movement seen beyond the NW limb in GOES SUVI 284 starting around 2026-06-04T03:30Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement chosen because this CME is only visible in a single coronagraph and because the source is far-sided.

cmes.note: Faint, narrow CME first seen to the SSW in STEREO A COR2 beginning around 2026-06-04T06:23Z. It is also seen to the SSW in SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1. The CME is mostly missed by SOHO LASCO C2 because the CME start time is covered by a short data gap. The source of this CME is likely an eruption with southward-directed ejecta seen near the W limb in GOES SUVI 304 beginning around 2026-06-04T04:40Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on best fit among SOHO LASCO C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2.

cmes.note: Faint CME first seen to the SW in GOES CCOR-1 starting around 2026-06-04T10:00Z. This front is also seen to the SW in STEREO A COR2 but was missed by SOHO LASCO C2/C3 in real time due to a downlink gap. The source is most likely some ejecta seen leaving the western limb in GOES SUVI 304 beginning around 2026-06-04T08:52Z. This is one in an hours long series of similar eruptions.

cmeAnalyses.note: Approximate measurement based on best fit between STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1. There is a higher degree of uncertainty in this measurement due to the faintness of the front.

cmes.note: Fast, narrow CME first seen to the SW in STEREO A COR2 and to the S in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery. A potential source is visible as moving/opening field lines along the southern limb in GOES SUVI 284 after the start time of the CME from STEREO A's perspective. Additionally, there is an eruption visible along/just beyond the SW limb in STEREO A EUVI 195 starting around 2026-06-04T16:35Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

cmes.note: Faint CME observed to the W in SOHO LASCO C2, fading into background after several frames. The source is likely AR 14458, with field line movement and dimming starting around 2026-06-04T20:22Z in SDO AIA 193 centered around S07W23. Additional dimming centered ~S25E30 triggered shortly after the first eruption.

cmeAnalyses.note: SOHO LASCO C2 only 3D analysis using the observed source longitude centered around W23, as such the parameters could vary slightly. CME fades after several frames in C2's field-of-view.

cmes.note: Faint CME observed to the W in SOHO LASCO C2/3 and GOES CCOR-1, fading into background late in LASCO C2 and early in LASCO C3/GOES CCOR-1. The source is likely AR 14458, with field line movement and dimming starting around 2026-06-04T22:40Z in SDO AIA 193 centered around S03W17.

cmeAnalyses.note: SOHO LASCO C2 only 3D analysis using the observed source longitude centered around W17, as such the parameters could vary slightly. CME fades quickly in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1. CME half width may be slightly below 10 degrees.

cmes.note: CME observed to the NW only in STEREO A COR2, perhaps due to the occulting disks of LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 obscuring visibility. No distinct source for this front is observed outside of some weak filament movement, without clearly erupting, near STEREO A's southwest limb in STEREO A EUVI 195 spanning approximately 2026-06-05T02:55Z - 2026-06-05T07:25Z. This front is quickly overlapped by significant CME: 2026-06-05T09:09Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: STEREO A only plane of sky, taken at W149.9 with STEREO A's current location at W59.9.

cmes.note: Far-sided halo CME to the SE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1, and wide front observed to the W in STEREO A COR2. The source is far-sided from Earth and likely STEREO A (W59.9) as well, should be well observed by Solar Orbiter (E156.3) when its data is available. The source is more directly observed in STEREO A EUVI 195/304 on or beyond STEREO's limb (~W149.9) as a filament eruption and field line movement starting around 2026-06-05T08:15Z. It is also observed in GOES SUVI 195/284, and less prominent in SDO AIA 171/193/211 with its closer field-of-view, as wide opening field lines and EUV wave over the SE limb starting around 2026-06-05T09:00Z. Latest Solar Orbiter EUI 304 imagery at 2026-06-0500:01Z shows numerous filaments resting around the W180-E150 and N15-S30 range surrounding former Active Regions 14439 and 14442, for potential candidates. Likely associated with an estimated M3 [M2,M6] flare observed by Solar Orbiter STIX peaking at 2026-06-05T08:58Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Triangulated bulk between SOHO LASCO C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 (which only has 1 image every hour due to ongoing campaign). Good confidence in fit near E160, E155-W165 maximum range based on fit shape.

Triangulated shock front between SOHO LASCO C3, GOES CCOR-1, and one available frame of STEREO A COR2 where the shock is visible. The shock front is complex and has two distinct edges, this analysis is of the "brighter" SE-directed inner shock (ahead of and distinct from the bright LE), excluding a faint more SW-directed shock edge as fitting both in one does not yield a good fit.

Triangulated shock front between SOHO LASCO C3 and one available frame of STEREO A COR2 where the shock is visible. The shock front is complex and has two distinct edges, this analysis is of the "more faint" SW-directed outer shock, excluding a bright more SE-directed shock edge as fitting both in one does not yield a good fit.

cmes.note: Faint CME front observed to the NW only in STEREO A COR2. No clear source for this CME is observed in SDO AIA, GOES SUVI, or STEREO A EUVI, although it could be outflow following significant CME: 2026-06-05T09:09Z from a far-sided filament eruption that triangulated around E160 earlier in the day.

cmeAnalyses.note: STEREO A plane-of-sky taken at W149.9, with STEREO A currently located at W59.9.

cmes.note: Faint CME front observed to the NE only in STEREO A COR2. No clear source for this CME is observed in SDO AIA, GOES SUVI, or STEREO A EUVI.

cmeAnalyses.note: STEREO A plane-of-sky taken at E30.1, with STEREO A currently located at W59.9.

cmes.note: Faint CME to the NW in SOHO LASCO C2 and GOES CCOR-1, and NE in STEREO A COR2, pylon obscured in LASCO C3. The source is a C4.1 flare from AR 14455 which peaked at 2026-06-05T15:03Z observed in SDO AIA 131, also observed as field line movement and small dimming in SDO AIA 171/193.

cmeAnalyses.note: Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2. The CME appears deflected northward and tracks askew with the swpc_cat tool, as such the parameters could vary slightly, with high confidence in the out-of-ecliptic direction.

cmes.note: Narrow CME first seen to the ne by STEREO A COR2 beginning at 2026-06-05T16:09Z. This CME is either directed into the pylon or occulting disk in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1, and is therefore not clearly visible. There is no clear source that fits the CME seen for this event.

cmeAnalyses.note: STEREO A plane-of-sky measurement using a longitude of E30.1 (as STEREO A is currently located at W59.9) due to lack of clear source an coronagraph imagery to triangulate.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the SW by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-06-05T23:24Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. This CME is not seen in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap which began at 2026-06-05T19:09Z. The source of this event is field line opening seen along the SW limb in SDO AIA 171 beginning at 2026-06-05T22:00Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement using a longitude of W90 due to source location near the limb and lack of SOHO/GOES coronagraph imagery to triangulate.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the southeast in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery. The source is likely far-sided as there are no source signatures on the Earth-facing disk around the time of this event.

cmeAnalyses.note: A plane-of-sky measurement was made using a longitude of -90 degrees due to the far-sided and therefore unknown source location.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the north in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and to the northeast in STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source is unknown, but may be related to faint field line movement visible over the northeast limb in SDO AIA 171 starting around 2026-06-06T03:00Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the southwest in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source is a far-sided eruption visible on the southwest limb in STEREO A EUVI 304 imagery starting around 2026-06-06T13:15Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

cmes.note: This CME is visible as a southeast partial halo in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery, and is seen to the east in STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source is an eruption and M1.8 flare from Active Region 14461 (S22E24) starting around 2025-06-06T13:45Z as seen in SDO AIA 131/171/193/304, GOES SUVI 131/171/195/284/304, and STEREO A EUVI 195/304 imagery. An opening of field lines is visible over the southeast limb in STEREO A EUVI 195 imagery, and a large EUV wave and dimming is observed surrounding the eruption site in SDO AIA 193 and GOES SUVI 195/284 imagery. Additionally, post eruptive arcades are visible in SDO AIA 171/193 and GOES SUVI 171/105 imagery starting around 2026-06-06T15:00Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Updated bulk measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Shock measurement following the outermost faint feature observed in GOES CCOR-1 imagery. A default half-width of 45 degrees was used as the shock feature is not observed in STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Preliminary bulk measurement based on the source longitude.

cmes.note: Faint CME observed to the E in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1, not seen in STEREO A COR2. The source may be field line movement in SDO AIA starting around 2026-06-06T16:34Z over the E limb around S10, or AR 14464 with slow brightening near S09E63, the movement is not too clear where it traces on-disk.

cmeAnalyses.note: SOHO LASCO C2/C3 3D analysis using E63 as the likely observed source longitude.

cmes.note: CME observed to the S in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1, not in STEREO A COR2 during a data gap spanning 2026-06-06T19:09Z to 2026-06-06T23:09Z. The source is a filament eruption far-sided from Earth, observed in STEREO A EUVI 304 at 2026-06-06T19:15Z as filament ejecta on/near its west limb, putting it at approximately W150 with STEREO A currently located at W60. Also observed as filament ejecta over the S limb in GOES SUVI 304.

cmeAnalyses.note: Speculative analysis of the shock front associated with this CME, using only GOES CCOR-1 due to its good view of the shock front and lack of STEREO A COR2 to triangulate with. W150 is used as the approximate source of the observed filament, and the width could greatly vary as well due to the partial halo single spacecraft analysis.

SOHO LASCO C2/C3 only fit due to the lack of STEREO A COR2 visibility to triangulate. W150 is used as the approximate source of the observed filament, and the lat/lon could vary. Higher confidence in the southward deflection out of the ecliptic plane, regardless.

cmes.note: Bright, relatively narrow CME seen to the southeast in SOHO LASCO C2, C3, and STEREO A COR2A, along with GOES CCOR-1 likely associated with a far-sided eruption.

cmeAnalyses.note: Leading edge measurement using all available spacecraft for triangulation of the longitude.

cmes.note: Narrow CME seen to the southeast seen in STEREO Ahead with seemingly no corresponding match in GOES and SOHO coronagraphs, likely associated with a far-sided eruption relative to the Earth-Sun line.

cmeAnalyses.note: STEREO Ahead plane of sky with just a few frames.

cmes.note: Minor CME seen to the northeast in a few frames of STEREO Ahead coronagraphs before it quickly disappears with unknown originating source.

cmeAnalyses.note: STEREO Ahead plane of sky measurement, as the source is unknown and the CME is not well observed in SOHO or GOES coronagraphs.

cmes.note: CME characterized by a wide, dark front seen to the west in STEREO Ahead, SOHO, and GOES coronagraphs with unknown source, perhaps in association with eruptive activity taking place over the STEREO Ahead western limb.

cmeAnalyses.note: Leading edge using three spacecraft (SOHO, STEREO Ahead, GOES) for triangulation of the longitude. High uncertainty in longitude with error in longitude up to +/- 30 degrees.

cmes.note: Faint CME first seen to the South by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-06-07T19:00Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. This event is not clearly seen in STEREO A COR2 due to a reduced campaign cadence. The source of this event is faint dimming seen centered at approx. S40E20 beginning at approx. 2026-06-07T18:25Z as seen in SDO AIA 193 and 211.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: Faint CME seen to the South by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-06-07T22:36Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. This CME is not clearly seen in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap which began at 2026-06-07T20:08Z. The source of this event is minor dimming and field line movement seen centered at approx. S40W20 beginning at approx. 2026-06-07T21:30Z as seen in SDO AIA 171, 193 and 211.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASC C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: CME observed to the SW in STEREO A COR2, and to the SSE in SOHO LASCO C2 and faintly in SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1. The source is likely on or near STEREO A's SW limb, currently around W150 with STEREO A at W60.2, observed in STEREO A EUVI 304 as filament ejecta starting around 2026-06-08T01:45Z. Although a bright spot on-disk is briefly observed with this filament ejecta on the limb, around W150, triangulation puts the front around E178, so the source is either further around the limb or deflected.

cmeAnalyses.note: Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.

cmes.note: Faint CME observed to the E in SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2, even more faintly in SOHO LASCO C3/GOES CCOR-1 due to it fading into the background after early frames. The source is likely an area of field line movement and dimming centered around S08E42, near AR 14464 (S12E35) starting around 2026-06-08T04:25Z in SDO AIA 171/193.

cmeAnalyses.note: Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2, noting the poor image quality in STEREO A COR2 due on an ongoing campaign along with the faint leading edge providing some uncertainty.

cmes.note: CME observed to the SW in STEREO A COR2 and SSW in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1. The source is very likely far-sided from Earth due to triangulation, likely visible in STEREO A EUVI 195/304 at 2026-06-08T07:25Z as field line dimming and movement on or near its SW limb, which is located around W150 with STEREO A at W60.3.

cmeAnalyses.note: Bulk triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and two available frames of STEREO A COR2. Front appears slightly more wide in COR2A than C2/C3.

Shock front triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2. With the poor image quality in STEREO A COR2 due to a campaign, W162 is used as the bulk longitude source and varying the parameters from there. Tracking the width via CCOR-1's view of the SE/S/SW expanding faint shock front, but the width could vary due to the uncertainty.
